Asynchronous RTK positioning enhanced by satellite-based PPP
corrections: a case study with PPP-B2b
Hong Huang1,2 · Wang Gao1,2 · Shuguo Pan1,2 · Longlei Qiao1,2 · Qing Zhao1,2
Received: 17 June 2025 / Accepted: 12 November 2025 / Published online: 22 November 2025
© The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag GmbH Germany, part of Springer Nature 2025
Abstract
GNSS network RTK is widely used in autonomous driving and precision mapping due to its high accuracy and coverage.

biases can still be largely eliminated by inter-station differ-
encing, exerting only a minor influence on ARTK position-
ing accuracy (Guo et al. 2015; Zeng et al. 2023).
To mitigate the degradation caused by such residual
errors, this study develops a high-precision ARTK frame-
work enhanced by satellite-based PPP corrections, with
BDS-3 PPP-B2b selected as a representative implementa-
tion (hereafter referred to as B2b-ARTK). The method sys-
tematically models and compensates for four primary error
sources: orbit errors, clock bias errors, ionosphe

GPS Solutions (2026) 30:30 Page 9 of 18 30
As shown in Fig. 4, the number of available satellites in the
static dataset remained relatively stable, with an average of
14.4 satellites visible and the position dilution of precision
(PDOP) consistently below 2.0. In contrast, the dynamic
dataset is more affected by environmental variations, with
correspondingly higher PDOP values, and the average num-
ber of visible satellites was 12.5.
Broadcast versus PPP-B2b orbit and clock accuracy
assessment
To assess the impact of PPP-B2b products on enhanc-
ing orbit and clock accuracy for ARTK positioning

30 Page 10 of 18 GPS Solutions (2026) 30:30
Fig. 6 Comparison of clock bias errors between broadcast and PPP-B2b ephemeris for GPS (left) and BDS-3 (right)
PPP-B2b corrections, the standard deviation of GPS clock
bias errors is reduced from 0.055 to 0.042 m, while that of
BDS-3 is significantly reduced from 0.164 to 0.033 m.
To further highlight the short-term behavior of PPP-B2b
corrections, a local time series of orbit and clock errors for a
representative BDS-3 satellite (C30) is shown in Fig. 7. The
results clearly reveal small periodic jumps: orbit correc-
tions exhibit discontinuities co

Fig. 9 Positioning performance of Brdc-ARTK and B2b-ARTK models under different constant data age conditions
also reflected in the comparison of post-fit residuals: the
improvement for the BDS-3 system is modest (reduced from
0.019 to 0.013 m), whereas the GPS system shows a more
pronounced residual reduction (from 0.030 to 0.012 m).
Overall, the experimental results validate that the
improvement of broadcast ephemeris orbit and clock accu-
racy via PPP-B2b effectively reduces residual errors in
ARTK and significantly enhances positioning performance

GPS Solutions (2026) 30:30 Page 13 of 18 30
Fig. 11 Comparison of post-fit carrier-phase residuals between B2b-ARTK and Brdc-ARTK models at 60-s data age
with data age increasing continuously over time, while
the B2b-PPP model continues continuous positioning. As
shown in Fig. 12, after the network outage, the positioning
error divergence rate of B2b-ARTK is significantly lower
than that of Brdc-ARTK, demonstrating its stronger capabil-
ity to resist error accumulation.
